Ken Belson, [Every Day is Sunday: ](http://marginalrevolution.com?action=user_content_redirect&uuid=dd56fd7e8472e28ceec416e64f152f734fb8229f005c5368f441b40e98a3af2d&blog_id=42693868&post_id=92058&user_id=262258391&subs_id=225460721&signature=6a6506236928a27dcb517cf300252924&email_name=new-post&user_email=ishan.mail@gmail.com&encoded_&email_id=75b5d05f24df8ee106e801a86382f85b)
Tom MacTague, [Between the Waves: The Hidden History of a Very British Revolution 1945-2016](http://marginalrevolution.com?action=user_content_redirect&uuid=6a5ebd5366f62e8162aa46ab416862beeade63524b26d22c8ba6a342b11659e8&blog_id=42693868&post_id=92058&user_id=262258391&subs_id=225460721&signature=fa223ff550addbbe6af66e942b6628a4&email_name=new-post&user_email=ishan.mail@gmail.com&encoded_&email_id=75b5d05f24df8ee106e801a86382f85b).
Both are excellent. I didn't read the first one right off, because I do not care very much about the topic. The book is good enough to overcome that problem. I did not read the second one right off because I care about the topic a lot, but thought I already knew enough about it. The book is good enough to overcome that problems.
